ourStory.title: Born in the Atlas Mountains
ourStory.body1: This story didn't start with us. Our father, Lahsen Aitidar, was among the first licensed Berber mountain guides in the High Atlas, leading trekkers through Toubkal and the Atlas valleys long before Morocco had a formal tourism industry. He learned every route by walking it, and he taught us the same way.
ourStory.body2: Smail and Mohamed grew up on those trails. In 2010 they formalised what had always been a family calling: building a proper tour operation on their father's foundation, expanding the routes and the standard of care, without ever moving from the one principle he lived by. Treat every guest as you would your own family.
ourStory.teamSubtitle: Born in the mountains, trained in the field, passionate about Morocco.

about.pageSubtitle: Born in the Atlas. Built for the world.
about.introBody: We're a family guiding operation from the High Atlas, not a booking platform. Every route on this site has been walked, tested, and refined by the same people who lead it.
about.promiseTitle: No Shortcuts. No Greenwashing. No Middlemen.
about.pillar1Body: Every guide on our team was born or raised in Morocco's mountain communities. We don't hire staff from travel agencies — we work with the people who actually know these mountains, valleys, and deserts by name.
about.pillar2Body: A significant portion of every booking goes directly to the local families, mule handlers, and guesthouses we work with. We don't use large hotel chains or international catering — we keep money in the communities we visit.
about.pillar3Body: When you book directly with us, you pay us — not a commission layer on top. That means better prices for you and better income for our guides. We've deliberately stayed off the big aggregators since day one.
about.promiseQuote: We don't just show you Morocco. We take you into it — the same way we've been doing it for the people we love.
about.promiseBody: Every tour we offer is one we've done ourselves, with our own families, before we ever offered it to a guest. That's the standard we hold ourselves to.
about.storyTitle1: We didn't start a tour company.
about.storyTitle2: We shared what we already knew.
about.storyBody1: This didn't start with us. Our father, Lahsen Aitidar, was one of the first licensed Berber mountain guides in the High Atlas — leading trekkers through Toubkal and the Atlas valleys long before Morocco had a formal tourism industry. He learned every route by walking it, and he taught us the same way.
about.storyBody2: We — Smail and Mohamed — grew up on those trails. In 2010 we took over and built on what he gave us: adding routes, adding rigour, building a proper operation. But the principle was already there: know the land deeply, treat every guest like family, never cut corners where safety or honesty is concerned.
about.storyBody3: Over the years since, we've expanded from Toubkal to the Sahara, from Marrakech to Agadir, from one guide to a full team of licensed professionals. Every one of us grew up walking the routes we now lead.
about.storyBody4: That's not a marketing line. It's a family legacy.
about.valueLocalBody: Guides born and raised in the landscapes they lead.
about.valueHonestBody: What you see is what you get — no hidden extras, no detours to souvenir shops.
about.valueGroupsBody: Private departures and true small groups. Never a bus tour in disguise.
about.valueTraceBody: Pack-in, pack-out on every trek. We partner with local cleanup crews in the Atlas.
about.milestone1Body: Our father, Lahsen Aitidar, was among the first licensed Berber mountain guides in the High Atlas. Long before tourism existed in the region, he was leading small groups through Toubkal, Ourika, and the Aït Benhaddou valley — building trust with travellers one route at a time.
about.milestone2Body: We grew up on these trails. In 2010, we — Smail and Mohamed — formalised what had always been a family calling: registering officially and building on everything our father taught us, while expanding the range of routes and the standard of service.
about.milestone3Body: We extended south, building our first Erg Chebbi desert camp partnership with a Berber family near Merzouga. The 3-day Marrakech–Sahara circuit became our most-requested trip within a year.
about.milestone4Body: Demand from the Atlantic coast led us to open a second base in Agadir — unlocking the Anti-Atlas, Paradise Valley, and Souss-Massa routes for travellers who didn't want to start from Marrakech.
about.milestone5Body: Today we run {tourCount} tours across four categories and six languages. The routes have grown. The standard hasn't moved. Every trip is one we'd take our own families on — because many of them, we already have.

contact.pageSubtitle: We're ready to help plan your perfect adventure.
contact.responseValue: WhatsApp: within 24 hrs · Email: may take longer
contact.formSuccess: Message sent! For the fastest reply, message us on WhatsApp. Email responses may take a little longer.
contact.formError: Something went wrong. Please try again or WhatsApp us directly.
contact.formMessagePlaceholder: Tell us about your dream adventure — dates, group size, experience level, any questions...
contact.formConsentPrefix: I agree that my details will be used to respond to my enquiry, in line with the
contact.messageSentBody: Thank you, {name}. We'll reply to {email} — WhatsApp replies come within 24 hours, email may take a little longer.
contact.customTripNudgeBody: Tell us your dates, group size, and what matters most — we'll build an itinerary around you.
contact.faq1A: Yes — all of our tours can be arranged as private departures. Contact us with your group size and preferred dates and we'll build a custom package.
contact.faq2Q: How do I pay the deposit?
contact.faq2A: We accept PayPal deposits to secure your booking. Full payment is due 14 days before your departure date.
contact.faq3Q: What languages do your guides speak?
contact.faq3A: Our guides speak Arabic, Berber (Tamazight), French, and English. Spanish-speaking guides available on request.
contact.faq4A: Free cancellation up to 14 days before departure. Cancellations within 14 days are subject to a 50% fee. No-shows are charged in full.
contact.faq5A: Travel insurance is not required but strongly recommended, especially for trekking tours. We suggest a policy that covers trip cancellation, medical expenses, and emergency evacuation.
contact.faq6Q: What group sizes do you accommodate?
contact.faq6A: Our tours run with a minimum of 2 participants. We offer both shared-group and fully private departures. Contact us with your group size and preferred dates and we'll build the right package — no upper limit for private bookings.
contact.faq7Q: What fitness level do I need?
contact.faq7A: It depends on the tour. Day trips and cultural tours suit all fitness levels. Trekking tours (Toubkal, High Atlas multi-day) require a good baseline of fitness — ability to walk 6–8 hours with a daypack. Each tour page has a difficulty rating to help you choose.
contact.faq8Q: Do I need a visa to visit Morocco?
contact.faq8A: Citizens of the EU, UK, USA, Canada, Australia, and most Western countries do not need a visa for stays under 90 days. Your passport should be valid for at least 6 months beyond your travel dates.
contact.faq9Q: What should I pack for a Morocco tour?
contact.faq9A: Light, breathable clothing for city and valley tours; warm layers for mountain treks. Modest dress is appreciated in medinas and villages. Good walking shoes are essential for almost every tour. A full packing list is included in your booking confirmation.
contact.faq10A: Yes. Please let us know about dietary requirements (vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, halal, allergies) when booking and we will arrange suitable meals. Moroccan cuisine is naturally vegetarian-friendly.
contact.faq11Q: Are your tours suitable for families with children?
contact.faq11A: Many of our day tours and cultural tours are excellent for families with children aged 6 and above. Trekking tours are better suited to older children and teenagers.
contact.faq12Q: What happens if weather forces a change of plan?
contact.faq12A: Safety is always our first priority. If severe weather makes a planned route dangerous, our guides will adapt the itinerary to a safe alternative. In the rare case a tour must be cancelled entirely due to weather, you will receive a full refund or the option to reschedule.

destinationsPage.title: Where the Adventures Happen
destinationsPage.subtitle: Eight extraordinary destinations across Morocco — each with its own character, climate, and story. Click any destination to discover tours, travel tips, and what makes it unmissable.
destinationsPage.readyBody: Our certified local guides are available for private and small-group tours year-round.

guidesPage.heroTitle: The people who actually know Morocco
guidesPage.heroSubtitle: Licensed Berber guides, born and raised in the landscapes they lead. Ask any traveller who's been to Morocco: the guide makes the trip. Meet ours.
guidesPage.trustLicensed: Officially licensed by Morocco's National Office for Tourism
guidesPage.trustTrained: Every guide trained on every route they lead
guidesPage.trustBornAtlas: Born in the Atlas — not hired from an agency
guidesPage.foundersBody: Brothers who grew up on these trails. They formalised the family guiding business in 2010 and have been building it ever since — one honest trip at a time.
guidesPage.legacyBody1: Every route we run today was walked first by Lahsen. The high passes of Toubkal, the mule tracks through Ourika, the stone-village paths of Aït Benhaddou — he mapped them in the 1980s, long before they appeared in any guidebook.
guidesPage.legacyBody2: Smail and Mohamed grew up walking beside him. When they took over in 2010, they inherited not just the routes, but the standard: deep local knowledge, total honesty with guests, and the belief that a guide's job is to make you feel the place, not just pass through it.
guidesPage.legacyBody3: That inheritance is what makes Marrakech Eco Tours different from any agency that hires freelance guides off a list.
guidesPage.storyBehindBody: Read the full history of how Lahsen's work in the 1980s became the foundation for everything Marrakech Eco Tours does today.
guidesPage.sectionTitle: The people who know this land by heart
guidesPage.sectionSubtitle: Not agency hires. Born and raised in the mountains, valleys, and medinas they lead.
guidesPage.lahsenBody: Father of Smail and Mohamed. One of the first licensed Berber mountain guides in the High Atlas (1980s). Every route we run today was walked first by him.
